caption: Figure 1: AMShortcut generates structurally accurate amorphous material samples with one or few sampling steps, enabling high- throughput inverse design of amorphous materials.
description2: The figure plots the generation of structurally accurate amorphous material samples using AMShortcut, which enables high-throughput inverse design with one or few sampling steps.
description3: Two-panel plot displays a sequence of amorphous material samples, progressing from random noise to noisy samples, and finally to generation.
questions: What type of material does the AMShortcut method facilitate the design of?
What is the general progression shown in the material's structural development?

caption: Figure 2: Distribution comparison of properties in the training samples versus the samples generated by AMShortcut with 100 steps. The target properties extend beyond the training distribution, demonstrating the model’s extrapolation capability.
description2: The figure plots a distribution comparison of properties between training samples and those generated by AMShortcut with 100 steps.
description3: Two-panel histograms compare training and generated sample distributions for shear modulus, ring size, modulus, lithium concentration, and shear modulus.
questions: How do the properties of generated samples compare to those of the training samples?
What is the distribution of properties observed in the generated samples?

caption: Figure 3: Structural accuracy evaluation for (a) RDF and (b) ADF of different models on the a-Si dataset with different numbers of sampling steps. ODE, SDE, and Shortcut corresponds to Material ODE, Material SDE, and AMShortcut, respectively.
description2: The figure plots structural accuracy evaluations for RDF and ADF of different models on the a-Si dataset with varying numbers of sampling steps.
description3: Three-panel plot displays line charts showing RDF and ADF data for different models and sampling steps, with x-axes labeled generically.
questions: How does the material sampling affect the accuracy of the evaluated models?
What structural features do the models show, and how do they correspond?

caption: Figure 4: RMSD of (a) RDF and (b) ADF versus generation time per 10,000 a-Si samples across different models and step counts. Labels indicate the number of sampling steps for each run.
description2: The figure plots a line chart comparing Root Mean Squared Difference (RMSD) of atomic density functions against generation time per 10,000 a-Si samples.
description3: Two-panel scatter plots display RMSD versus generation time per 10,000 a-Si samples, comparing RDF and ADF across models and step counts.
questions: How do different models affect the radial and angular distribution function RMSD over time?
What is the general relationship between generation time and RMSD for these models?

caption: Figure 6: Property MAPE versus generation time per 2,000 MEG samples across different models and step counts for (a) Young’s modulus, (b) Lithium molar concentration, and (c) shear modulus. Labels indicate the number of sampling steps for each run.
description2: The figure plots a line chart comparing Property MAPE versus generation time per 2,000 MEG samples across different models and step counts for Young's modulus, Lithium molar concentration, and shear modulus.
description3: Three-panel scatter plots display property MAPE versus generation time per 2,000 MEG samples, comparing models and step counts for Young’s modulus, lithium molar concentration, and shear modulus.
questions: How do different models perform in predicting material properties over time?
What is the general relationship between generation time and property prediction accuracy?

caption: Figure 5: Property MAPE versus generation time per 2,000 a-SiO2 samples across different models and step counts for (a) shear modulus and (b) ring size distribution. Labels indicate the number of sampling steps for each run.
description2: The figure plots the property MAPE against generation time per 2,000 a-SiO2 samples, comparing different models and step counts for shear modulus and ring size distribution.
description3: Two-panel plot displays property MAPE versus generation time for shear modulus and ring size distribution, using different colored markers to represent models and step counts.
questions: What main idea or comparison does the figure present?
What overall structure or pattern is shown in the figure?
